summary: PGO (Profile-Guided Optimization) implemented for MySQL 8.0.25 self-compiled binary on Kunpeng 920 (aarch64). Achieved 4.52% TPS improvement with reduced latency.

baseline_identity.lse_instructions: confirmed (2695 CAS instructions, LDADD instructions present in objdump)

pgo_binary_identity.training_workload: sysbench oltp_read_only, 10 tables x 1M rows, 32 threads, 120 seconds

arch_flag_gap: None. Current flags already use -march=armv8.2-a+crc+crypto+lse -mtune=native which matches Kunpeng 920 (CPU implementer 0x48, CPU part 0xd01, ARMv8.2 with LSE/CRC/NEON/DotProduct). Available CPU features (atomics, crc32, asimd, asimddp, dcpop) are all matched by current arch flags.

lse_atomics.detail: LSE LDADD, CAS, SWP instructions found. No load/store exclusive (ldxr/stxr) detected. LSE is fully utilized.

crc32.detail: Software CRC32 functions detected (ut_crc32, ut_crc32_cpu_enabled). Hardware CRC instructions available in CPU. MySQL uses HW CRC when available via runtime dispatch.

lto.detail: LTO enabled via -flto=auto. Binary size reduced from 209MB (without LTO) to 68MB (with LTO + PGO).

profile_guided_candidates.name: PGO with GCC 10.3.1
profile_guided_candidates.notes: PGO implemented. Measured 4.52% gain from local A/B testing. The gain is at the lower end of the expected range, likely because: (1) baseline already has LTO + full arch flags which capture much of the optimization potential; (2) training workload was sysbench oltp_read_only (read-only) which doesn't cover write code paths; (3) local benchmark uses 32 threads vs remote 64+ threads which changes profiling characteristics.

benchmark_results.methodology: A/B comparison on same server (192.168.90.170) with sysbench running locally. MySQL deployed on cpuset 64-79,88-95 (Node 2). Binary swapped between baseline and PGO-optimized with cold restart. 60s runs, 32 threads, oltp_read_only.

pgo_optimized.binary: PGO GCC 10.3.1 (profile-generate + profile-use, O3, LTO, arch flags)

candidate_actions.precondition: Source and build infrastructure available at /home/mysql/mysql-server-mysql-8.0.25, GCC 10.3.1, sysbench accessible
candidate_actions.description: Complete PGO cycle: (1) cmake with -fprofile-generate (no LTO), (2) make -j128, (3) deploy to /usr/local/mysql-opt-pgo/, (4) start on port 3308, (5) run sysbench training (120s, 32 threads), (6) cmake with -fprofile-use + -fprofile-correction + LTO, (7) make -j128, (8) deploy, (9) benchmark
candidate_actions.validation: A/B benchmark: baseline 9,777 TPS -> PGO 10,219 TPS

further_compiler_optimization_potential.notes: GCC 12.2.1 available at /opt/openEuler/gcc-toolset-12/root/usr/bin/gcc. Provides better auto-vectorization, better LTO, improved ARM tuning. Previous GCC 12 build failed (pre-existing build config issue, not compiler failure). GCC 12 + PGO combined could yield additional gains.

Requires LLVM 12+ tools (llvm-bolt, perf2bolt). Not currently installed. BOLT post-processes the compiled binary using perf data to optimize code layout, reducing I-cache misses. The high L1 I-cache misses (11.5B/10s) suggest BOLT could help.

Current training only used oltp_read_only. Additional training with oltp_write_only or oltp_read_write would cover InnoDB write path functions (log system, flush, page write) which PGO could further optimize.

Uses perf hardware sampling instead of instrumentation-based PGO. Avoids the need for -fprofile-generate build. GCC 10 supports -fauto-profile. Requires perf record with LBR, which Kunpeng 920 may not fully support.

binary_equivalence_check.notes: Only difference is compiler flags (PGO instrumentation and profile-guided optimization). LTO maintained in final build. Source identical.

notes: Current running MySQL uses PGO-optimized binary (hash 34d1362b...). Baseline saved as mysqld.baseline.
build-opt directory from original build preserved at /home/mysql/mysql-8.0.25-build-opt/
PGO build directories preserved: pgo-build-v4 (instrumentation), pgo-build-final (final optimized)
